html:
Exclusive to:

Toma Root is a Vegetable Food Item in Don't Starve Together. Toma Root can be Farmed with a moderate chance from planting Seeds, or by using Spiky Seeds.

When eaten raw, it restores 3 Health and 12.5 Hunger. Toma Root takes 6 days to spoil. It can be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes and is required for Spicy Vegetable Stinger, Salsa Fresca and Moqueca. Feeding a Toma Root to a bird in a Birdcage will produce 1 Toma Root Seed, as of the "Reap What You Sow" update.

Roasted Toma Root

Roasted Toma Root is obtained by cooking a Toma Root over a Campfire, Fire Pit, or Star. It restores 17 more Health than the raw version and takes 10 days to spoil. It has the same uses as raw Toma Root.

 Usage

Recipes

×2

×2

Fillers can not be Inedibles, Meats or Eggs.
Fillers cannot be Inedible.

The Gorge

Only during the Event: The Gorge
Toma Roots are featured in The Gorge event in Don't Starve Together. They grow from Spiky Seeds, which can be bought from Pipton for one Old Coin. A Toma Root can be made into a Roast Toma Root by being cooked at the Campfire next to the Swamp Pig Elder. Toma Roots can be used in a variety of Cooking Recipes exclusive to The Gorge.

Recipes

×2

×2

Trivia

  • The Toma Root was originally exclusive to The Gorge event, but it was later introduced into the main game in the Warly Character Update.
  • The binomical name Wickerbottom gives for the fruit and its seed, Solanum lycopersicum, is identical to that of normal tomatoes.
  • It is unclear how the developers settled on the name "toma root". The word "tomato" appears much more often in game text than the phrase "toma root", appearing in examination quotes, the item description of Rude Interlude, and the Plant Registry "field note" of the Toma Root plant.
    • One potential explanation for their name could be the fact that all crops would grow within soil in The Gorge event, and since Toma Roots resemble Tomatoes, which do not grow within the ground directly but rather from a vine, they settled on the name "Toma Root".

Gallery

  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Rễ Toma là một loại thực phẩm rau củ trong Don't Starve Together. Rễ Toma có thể được trồng với tỉ lệ vừa phải bằng cách gieo Hạt giống hoặc bằng cách sử dụng Hạt giống Gai.

'''Toma Root''' is a [[Vegetable]] [[Food]] [[Item]] in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]''. Toma Root can be [[Farming|Farmed]] with a moderate chance from planting [[Seeds]], or by using [[Crop Seeds/DST|Spiky Seeds]].

When eaten raw, it restores 3 [[Health]] and 12.5 [[Hunger]]. Toma Root takes 6 days to spoil. It can be used as an ingredient in several Crock Pot recipes and is required for [[Spicy Vegetable Stinger]], [[Salsa Fresca]] and [[Moqueca]]. Feeding a Toma Root to a bird in a [[Birdcage]] will produce 1 [[Crop Seeds|Toma Root Seed]], as of the "Reap What You Sow" update.

== {{Pic|32|Silver Bowl}} The Gorge ==
{{Event|The Gorge}}
Toma Roots are featured in [[The Gorge]] [[:Category:Events|event]] in ''Don't Starve Together''. They grow from Spiky Seeds, which can be bought from [[Pipton]] for one Old Coin. A Toma Root can be made into a Roast Toma Root by being cooked at the [[Campfire]] next to the [[Swamp Pig Elder]]. Toma Roots can be used in a variety of [[The Gorge Recipes|Cooking Recipes]] exclusive to The Gorge.

== {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The Toma Root was originally exclusive to ''The Gorge'' event, but it was later introduced into the main game in the [[Warly]] [[Don't Starve Together/Version History|Character Update]].
* The binomical name [[Wickerbottom]] gives for the fruit and its seed, ''Solanum lycopersicum'', is identical to that of normal [[Wikipedia:tomato|tomatoes]].
* It is unclear how the developers settled on the name "toma root". The word "tomato" appears much more often in game text than the phrase "toma root", appearing in examination quotes, the item description of [[Battle_Songs#Rude_Interlude|Rude Interlude]], and the [[Plant Registry]] "field note" of the Toma Root plant. 
**One potential explanation for their name could be the fact that all crops would grow within soil in ''The Gorge'' event, and since Toma Roots resemble Tomatoes, which do not grow within the ground directly but rather from a vine, they settled on the name "Toma Root".
